without_it.dafny_error: function precondition could not be proved (SeqMin requires |a|>0); upper bound below lower bound on a[..i]; FeasibilityLemma preconditions

diagnosis.explanation: Without bounds on i, Z3 cannot prove that a[..i] is well-formed (requires 0 <= i <= |a|) or that SeqMin(a[..i]) is well-defined (requires |a[..i]| > 0).
diagnosis.smt_gap: none — this is a standard logical necessity

without_it.dafny_error: FeasibilityLemma precondition: lo == SeqMin(a) could not be proved

diagnosis.explanation: Without tracking that lo == SeqMin of the prefix, there is no connection between the loop variable lo and the spec function SeqMin.
diagnosis.smt_gap: none — this is a standard logical necessity

without_it.dafny_error: FeasibilityLemma precondition: hi == SeqMax(a) could not be proved

diagnosis.explanation: Without tracking that hi == SeqMax of the prefix, there is no connection between the loop variable hi and the spec function SeqMax.
diagnosis.smt_gap: none — this is a standard logical necessity

without_it.dafny_error: invariant lo == SeqMin(a[..i]) could not be proved to be maintained by the loop; invariant hi == SeqMax(a[..i]) could not be proved to be maintained by the loop

diagnosis.explanation: SeqMin/SeqMax unfold recursively on a[..i+1], producing SeqMin(a[..i+1][..|a[..i+1]|-1]) = SeqMin(Seq#Take(Seq#Take(a, i+1), i)). To connect this to the old invariant SeqMin(Seq#Take(a, i)), Z3 must prove Seq#Take(Seq#Take(a, i+1), i) == Seq#Take(a, i). No axiom exists for this simplification, despite the parallel Drop-of-Drop axiom existing.
diagnosis.smt_gap: Seq#Take(Seq#Take(s, m), n) has no simplification axiom to Seq#Take(s, n) when n <= m

proposed_axiom.boogie: axiom (forall s: Seq, m: int, n: int :: { Seq#Take(Seq#Take(s, m), n) } 0 <= n && n <= m && m <= Seq#Length(s) ==> Seq#Take(Seq#Take(s, m), n) == Seq#Take(s, n));
proposed_axiom.test_details: alone: 16/18 verified (fixes loop invariants, 2 pre-existing Boogie-level lemma errors remain); combined with take-of-full-length: 18/18 verified

without_it.dafny_error: FeasibilityLemma preconditions: lo == SeqMin(a) and hi == SeqMax(a) could not be proved

diagnosis.explanation: After loop exit with i == |a|, invariants give lo == SeqMin(a[..|a|]) and hi == SeqMax(a[..|a|]). FeasibilityLemma requires lo == SeqMin(a). Z3 cannot prove SeqMin(Seq#Take(a, Seq#Length(a))) == SeqMin(a) because there is no axiom stating Seq#Take(s, Seq#Length(s)) == s. The parallel Drop(s, 0) == s axiom does exist.
diagnosis.smt_gap: Seq#Take(s, Seq#Length(s)) has no simplification axiom to s

proposed_axiom.boogie: axiom (forall s: Seq :: { Seq#Length(s) } Seq#Take(s, Seq#Length(s)) == s);
proposed_axiom.test_details: alone: 15/18 verified (doesn't fix loop invariants); combined with take-of-take: 18/18 verified

without_it.dafny_error: postcondition MinStolenCount(a, stolen) could not be proved on return 0 path; FeasibleStolenCount not established (exists x :: ValidStoreConfig)

diagnosis.explanation: FeasibleStolenCount requires proving an existential (exists x :: ValidStoreConfig(a, x, |a|+k)). Z3 cannot find the witness x = SeqMin(a) without the lemma explicitly constructing it. The lemma calls SeqMinIsMin to prove all elements are >= lo, then asserts ValidStoreConfig(a, lo, |a|+k) to provide the witness.
diagnosis.smt_gap: none — existential witness construction is inherently difficult for SMT solvers

without_it.dafny_error: postcondition MinStolenCount(a, stolen) could not be proved on return res path; FeasibleStolenCount not established

diagnosis.explanation: Same mechanism as call_feasibility_0 — needs explicit witness construction for the existential in FeasibleStolenCount.

without_it.dafny_error: postcondition MinStolenCount(a, stolen) could not be proved; !FeasibleStolenCount(a, k-1) not established

diagnosis.explanation: MinStolenCount requires proving !FeasibleStolenCount(a, res-1), which means proving (forall x :: !ValidStoreConfig(a, x, |a|+res-1)). This requires a proof by contradiction for each x, using witnesses from SeqMinInSeq and SeqMaxInSeq to derive contradictions. Z3 cannot construct this argument without the lemma.
diagnosis.smt_gap: none — requires sophisticated universal quantifier reasoning with explicit contradiction

take_of_take_alone.note: Fixes loop invariants; 2 pre-existing Boogie-level lemma errors remain

take_of_full_length_alone.note: Doesn't fix loop invariants; only addresses post-loop connection

notes: This problem has 8 essential elements: 3 loop invariants, 2 assertions, and 3 lemma calls. The 3 invariants and 3 lemma calls are standard logical necessities (bounds tracking, accumulator invariants, existential witness construction, universal quantifier reasoning). The 2 assertions work around missing Boogie sequence axioms: (1) Take-of-Take (Seq#Take(Seq#Take(s,m),n) == Seq#Take(s,n)) and (2) Take-of-full-length (Seq#Take(s, Seq#Length(s)) == s). Both have parallels for Seq#Drop that DO exist in the prelude. Both proposed axioms pass testing when combined (18/18 VCs verified).
